core_model.structure_summary: A coupled system of first-order-in-time ordinary differential equations for interacting state vectors whose production rates are nonlinear learned surrogate functions and whose loss terms are elementwise dilution/degradation products, with the rates constrained by an embedded linear steady-state balance problem.
core_model.canonical_form: \dot{z} = b(t)\,q_z(z,e,\theta,\Theta), \qquad \dot{e} = q_e(u,\theta) - \big(\mu(z,e)\,\mathbf{1} + d(e)\big)\circ e, \qquad q_z = f_{\mathrm{NN}}(e,\Theta)\,h(z),\ \ \text{s.t. } S v = 0,\ v_{\min}\le v\le v_{\max}

variables.meaning: vectors of extensive quantities and internal catalytic species evolving in time
external manipulable input driving production rates
kinetic parameters and weights of the learned surrogate embedding the steady-state balance constraints

notes: The paper's central object is a hybrid dynamic model: a small system of first-order ODEs whose rate functions are machine-learning surrogates trained to reproduce an embedded constrained linear steady-state (flux-balance) optimization. Alternative core framings are the embedded constrained optimization itself or the outer optimal-control/estimation problems (MPC/MHE), but the ODE state model is the structural centerpiece the framework is built around. Loss terms (mu*1 + d)∘e give elementwise damping; production terms are driving.
